findings.message: D3 overstates the benefit: `max_over_layers(ceil(layer_work / ceiling_for_layer))` is not guaranteed to be smaller than the per-task serial `N` rounds, and the later Negative section already says a single-layer feature can become the bottleneck. This should be phrased as a typical reduction, not a universal one.

D5 says `/track:implement` already performs multi-task parallel implementation plus a single review and a single commit, but `implement.md` only covers implementation and task transitions. The review/commit steps belong to `/track:full-cycle` and `/track:commit`, so this rationale is built on the wrong command behavior.

IN-01 says the batch commits as soon as any layer's cumulative diff reaches the per-scope ceiling, but CN-01/AC-03 later say a layer may be at the ceiling and still continue adding tasks from other layers; the split is only required when the next task would make some layer exceed its ceiling. This leaves the plan with conflicting batch-boundary semantics and can make T005 implement an unnecessarily early split, reducing the intended round aggregation.

AC-04 requires the ceiling values to be adjustable by changing `.harness/config/review-scope.json` only, but its coverage maps solely to T002, which only adds/configures the JSON fields. The runtime pieces that make the setting effective are T003 (`ReviewScopeConfig` stores/returns the effective ceiling) and T004 (loader reads the JSON fields), so the coverage/rendered task list lets AC-04 be considered satisfied without the loader/domain support that the acceptance criterion itself requires.

`diff_ceiling_for_scope` is documented as returning the global default whenever a scope has no per-scope override, but T003 also requires `ScopeName::Other` to always return `None`. With `default_diff_ceiling` set, those two contracts conflict, so the implementation and tests for `Other` cannot satisfy both artifacts. Clarify whether the global default applies only to configured review scopes or whether `Other` should inherit it.
